_meta.description: Default-ON convergence dry run (sandboxed, production restored): iterate [resonator-aware 4-flag fit --write -> constant sync -> Stage-1 resonator refit] x4. RESULT: NO clean convergence — the coupled system oscillates between two regimes: A (deltaTStart ~58-59, Espenak ~17.1-17.3 s, Bond amp ~284-287, resonator tone amp balloons to -1250..-1343 s cancellation-heavy) and B (deltaTStart ~65.2-66.0, Espenak ~20.7-20.8 s, Bond amp ~246-250, tone ~-870..-949). Diagnosis: Bond (1,466 yr) and the resonator drive tone (3,695 yr) plus the deltaTStart constant are strongly collinear in the 2.7-kyr window — sequential iterate-and-close trades amplitude between them instead of converging. CONCLUSION: default-ON requires a JOINT fit (stack + resonator in one design matrix, shared USNO closure) with resonator amplitude CAPS at the convention values (tone -186 s, the flag-cap philosophy) — not sequential iteration. Espenak upside is real in both regimes (17.1-20.8 vs 22.5 production). Production files verified restored.
